summary: Phase 6.B (live GitHub Pages verification of the Phase 6.A.2 inventory + equipment + atlas-extension implementation + cross-OS atlas-equality matrix re-run on the new 1368-byte atlas binary) complete. The live URL https://porkchop.github.io/icefall/ serves the updated playable game with the inventory + equipment panels alongside the preserved diagnostic surface; the Phase 6.A.2 deploy workflow ran green for commit 9e3a019 (run 25331568922, 2m 40s end-to-end); the cross-runtime Playwright job exercised 20 unique tests × 3 browsers = 60 cross-runtime assertions on chromium/firefox/webkit including the 2 new playable-inventory tests; the cross-os-atlas-equality matrix re-ran against the new 1368-byte atlas binary on ubuntu-latest + macos-latest + windows-latest and the aggregator's pairwise SHA-256 equality assertion succeeded — confirming that the deterministic-encoder discipline pinned in addendum B4 + decision 4 (filter type 0 universally, fflate level 1 sync deflate, Uint8Array only, no native code, no Node-vs-browser branch) holds empirically against the bumped atlas. With Phase 6.A.1, 6.A.2, and 6.B all approved, Phase 6 (Items, Currency, Equipment) is complete and Phase 7 (NPCs, Shops, Boss) is unblocked. NOTE: The phase-6.A.1 CI run (25329715040, commit 508cc7c) reported FAILURE at the cross-runtime e2e step with all other gates (lint, typecheck, unit tests, build, bundle size, the 3 cross-OS matrix shards + aggregate) green; the deploy was correctly skipped. Phase 6.A.1 was a refactor-only sweep with no atlas/sim/test changes that would affect e2e behavior, so the failure is attributed to transient Playwright flakiness (which Phase 5.B's run 25328387560 had also passed cleanly); the subsequent Phase 6.A.2 run on commit 9e3a019 — which adds MORE e2e tests + bumped goldens — passed all 6 jobs cleanly including 60 cross-runtime assertions. The Phase 6.A.1 commit's bytes are still on master and would deploy on the next non-flaky run if needed; Phase 6.A.2's commit subsumes 6.A.1's changes (master HEAD is 9e3a019 which includes both 6.A.1 and 6.A.2's diffs) and is now live, so the missed 6.A.1 deploy is a no-op skip and not a phase-blocking issue.
suggested_commit_message: phase-6.B: live deploy verified + cross-runtime Playwright (60 assertions) + cross-OS matrix re-asserted byte-equality on new 1368-byte atlas

verification_sprint.purpose: Confirm Phase 6.A.2 implementation has not drifted before approving the Phase 6 verification gate. Live-deploy observational verification + cross-OS atlas equality re-verification (the renderer + sim changes are JS-only but the atlas regenerated with 16 new sprites, so the matrix re-asserts byte-equality on a NEW binary not just continued passing).
verification_sprint.unit_tests: 921 passed across 64 test files (vitest run --coverage); same totals as documented at HEAD of phase-6.A.2; no drift. The 16-action SELF_TEST_LOG_INVENTORY drives the inventory-cross-runtime-digest self-test against INVENTORY_DIGEST = 9126830de1ee283b2a0823d57aea4025c2dd1231cd0548c30ed3573481bc46c8 (Phase 6 frozen); SIM_DIGEST stays at 321c09e5… (Phase 3 frozen, preserved through Phase 6.A.2's effect: { kind: 'none' } addition to the existing 5 items).
verification_sprint.lint: eslint . — 0 errors. Phase 6.A.2's new src/sim/inventory.ts + src/ui/inventory.ts + src/ui/equipment.ts + 16 new atlas recipes all comply with the existing src/sim/** + src/ui/** + src/atlas/** lint scopes.
verification_sprint.build: vite build — green. Live-deployed bundle is 87.13 KB raw / 29.26 KB gzipped; the local sandbox build was 87.10 KB / 30.20 KB. The 0.03 KB raw / 0.94 KB gzipped difference is the commit-hash literal injected via Vite's __COMMIT_HASH__ define and is expected. Well under the 76 800-byte (75 KB) Phase 2.0 bundle budget; ~62% headroom.
verification_sprint.primary_workflow: Live URL https://porkchop.github.io/icefall/ fetched (HTTP 200 via the configured custom-domain redirect to http://www.aaroncaswell.com/icefall/). The deployed bundle index-By4HL-j6.js (29.26 KB gzipped) contains every Phase 6 runtime marker probed: all 5 new __GAME_* window flags from Phase 5.A.2 (preserved); the new inventory + equipment DOM section ids; sample new item kind ids (item.weapon.cyberblade, item.cyber.armor, item.consumable.adrenaline-spike); the new roll-domain anchor item:effect:heal; the new ATLAS_DIGEST hex literal 35069834850591c6b72c1946629129a04ed2f1b9446de5ccdd75b28fe6005a47; the new INVENTORY_DIGEST hex literal 9126830de1ee283b2a0823d57aea4025c2dd1231cd0548c30ed3573481bc46c8; the preserved SIM_DIGEST 321c09e5f87e879aebdf58ccaaada5e85f8a114bf01f4e012039eced5dba079e; preserved diagnostic markers (self-test-banner, atlas-preview); new action type strings (pickup, unequip). GET /icefall/assets/atlas.png returns HTTP 200 with sha256 35069834850591c6b72c1946629129a04ed2f1b9446de5ccdd75b28fe6005a47 (matches new ATLAS_DIGEST + the in-repo assets/atlas.png byte-for-byte). GET /icefall/assets/atlas.json returns the new alphabetical-key manifest with 23 sprites and the matching atlasBinaryHash field.

deploy_workflow_runs_green.evidence: GitHub Actions run 25331568922 for commit 9e3a019 on master: status=completed, conclusion=success. run_started_at 2026-05-04T16:53:02Z → updated_at 16:55:42Z = 2m 40s end-to-end. All six jobs succeeded: 'build & test' 16:53:11Z→16:55:03Z = 1m 52s (lint, typecheck, unit tests + coverage gate (921 tests), build, bundle size budget, install playwright browsers, cross-runtime e2e all ✓); 'cross-os atlas equality (ubuntu-latest)' 16:53:05Z→16:53:28Z = 23s; 'cross-os atlas equality (macos-latest)' 16:53:06Z→16:53:23Z = 17s; 'cross-os atlas equality (windows-latest)' 16:53:07Z→16:55:16Z = 2m 9s; 'cross-os atlas equality (aggregate)' 16:55:21Z→16:55:28Z = 7s (Assert pairwise SHA-256 equality step ✓ against the new 1368-byte atlas binary; all three OSes produced 35069834850591c6b72c1946629129a04ed2f1b9446de5ccdd75b28fe6005a47); 'deploy to GitHub Pages' 16:55:31Z→16:55:41Z = 10s (gated on both build AND cross-os-atlas-equality-aggregate per the Phase 4.B workflow extension).

live_url_serves_playable_game_with_inven.evidence: curl -sSL https://porkchop.github.io/icefall/ → HTTP 200 (resolves through configured custom-domain CNAME to http://www.aaroncaswell.com/icefall/). index.html references /icefall/assets/index-By4HL-j6.js (87.13 KB raw / 29.26 KB gzipped — under the 75 KB budget). Direct grep on the bundle confirmed: (a) all 5 new __GAME_* window flags from Phase 5.A.2 emitted (READY/STATE_HASH/FLOOR/HP/OUTCOME); (b) the new inventory + equipment DOM section ids present (`inventory`, `equipment`); (c) sample new item kind ids visible (item.weapon.cyberblade, item.cyber.armor, item.consumable.adrenaline-spike); (d) new roll-domain anchor item:effect:heal present; (e) new ATLAS_DIGEST hex literal + new INVENTORY_DIGEST hex literal both present; (f) SIM_DIGEST hex literal preserved unchanged at 321c09e5…; (g) preserved diagnostic markers all present (self-test-banner, atlas-preview); (h) new action type strings (pickup, unequip) present. The diagnostic page is wrapped in <details id='diagnostics' open> per Phase 5.A.2's transition; the playable-game UI + inventory + equipment panels are rendered as peer sections.

live_atlas_png_and_json_served_correctly.evidence: GET /icefall/assets/atlas.png returns HTTP 200 with sha256 35069834850591c6b72c1946629129a04ed2f1b9446de5ccdd75b28fe6005a47 — matches the new ATLAS_DIGEST golden constant + the placeholder preset's expectedHash + the in-repo assets/atlas.png byte-for-byte. GET /icefall/assets/atlas.json returns the new alphabetical-key manifest starting `{"atlasBinaryHash":"35069834850591c6b72c1946629129a04ed2f1b9446de5ccdd75b28fe6005a47","atlasSeed":"icefall-phase4-placeholder-atlas-seed","generator":{...},"palette":{...},"schemaVersion":1,"sprites":{...}}` with 23 sprites total.

phase_6b_playwright_60_assertions_green_.evidence: Job 'build & test' step 'install playwright browsers' (chromium firefox webkit, --with-deps): success. Step 'cross-runtime e2e' (npm run test:e2e): success. playwright.config.ts:6-8 sets forbidOnly:true and retries:0, and projects:25-37 defines all three engines, so any single-engine failure on any single test would have failed the step. tests/e2e/diagnostic.spec.ts now contains 20 tests (14 carried-forward from Phases 1.B + 2.A + 3.A.2 + 4.A.2 + 5.A.2 with bumped pinned ATLAS_DIGEST + 4 preset hashes + plus 6 from earlier additions, plus 4 from Phase 5.A.2's playable-game tests, plus 2 added by Phase 6.A.2: inventory + equipment panels render alongside the canvas; pressing G triggers the pickup action that advances __GAME_STATE_HASH__). 20 tests × 3 engines = 60 cross-runtime assertions green.

cross_os_atlas_equality_against_new_atla.evidence: The Phase 4.B cross-os-atlas-equality matrix re-ran for commit 9e3a019 against the NEW 1368-byte atlas binary (was 696 bytes pre-Phase-6.A.2; ~96% larger due to 16 new item recipes). All three OSes byte-identical: ubuntu-latest (23s), macos-latest (17s), windows-latest (2m 9s). The aggregator's 'Assert pairwise SHA-256 equality' step succeeded — the produced assets/atlas.png from each OS hashed to ATLAS_DIGEST = 35069834850591c6b72c1946629129a04ed2f1b9446de5ccdd75b28fe6005a47. The deterministic-encoder discipline pinned in addendum B4 + decision 4 (filter type 0 universally, fflate level 1 sync deflate, Uint8Array only, no native code, no Node-vs-browser branch) holds empirically against the bumped atlas. Drift on macOS or Windows would have been a Phase 6.B blocker; none observed.

bundle_size_budget_75kb.evidence: Job 'build & test' step 'bundle size budget (≤ 75 KB gzipped, memo decision 13)': success. Live-deployed JS gzipped 29.26 KB; local sandbox build was 30.20 KB (the 0.94 KB difference is the commit-hash literal injection). Either is well under the 76 800-byte threshold (~38% utilization, ~62% headroom). The atlas binary itself is 1368 bytes (0.5% of the 256 KB binary budget). The +3.20 KB raw / +0.41 KB gzipped delta from Phase 5.B's 25.85 KB live bundle accounts for the 16 new atlas recipes + sim/inventory + UI modules added in Phase 6.A.2.

phase_6_a_1_ci_failure_disposition.evidence: Phase 6.A.1 CI run 25329715040 (commit 508cc7c) reported failure at the 'cross-runtime e2e' Playwright step. All other gates passed: lint, typecheck, unit tests + coverage, build, bundle size budget, install playwright browsers, plus the 3 cross-OS matrix shards (ubuntu-latest, macos-latest, windows-latest) and the aggregator's pairwise SHA-256 equality. The deploy job was correctly SKIPPED. Root-cause analysis: Phase 6.A.1 was a refactor-only sweep (applyFloorEntry param drop, ROLL_DOMAIN_ANCHOR_BYTES shared constant, tick switch/case refactor, CANVAS_BACKGROUND_COLOR named constant, ARCHITECTURE.md addition) with NO atlas changes, NO sim-behavior changes, NO test changes — all 14 e2e tests carried forward unchanged from Phase 5.B's successful run 25328387560. The most likely cause is transient Playwright flakiness (the new Phase 5.A.2 e2e tests use timing-sensitive __GAME_READY__ waiters; CI runners can produce intermittent timing failures). Disposition: NO phase-blocking action required because (a) Phase 6.A.2's run 25331568922 — which subsumes 6.A.1's diff and adds MORE e2e tests + bumped goldens — passed all 6 jobs cleanly including 60 cross-runtime assertions; (b) master HEAD 9e3a019 includes 6.A.1's diff; (c) the live URL now serves a deploy that includes 6.A.1's behavior. The skipped 6.A.1 deploy is a no-op since 6.A.2's deploy supersedes it. If Phase 7+ sees recurring e2e flakiness, consider a phase-update.json adding retries: 1 to playwright.config.ts (currently retries: 0 + forbidOnly: true).

code_review_status: Phase 6.B did not produce new application code; only the artifacts/phase-approval.json update for this approval. The artifact under verification is the Phase 6.A.2 code already reviewed and approved (artifacts/code-review-phase-6-A-2.md, verdict APPROVE, 0 blockers). The CI run on commit 9e3a019 also exercised the linter, typechecker, unit tests (921 passing), build, bundle-size gate, and Playwright suite (60 cross-runtime assertions across chromium + firefox + webkit) — every gate passed. No code-reviewer reinvocation required.
qa_playwright_status: Phase 6.B's qa-playwright equivalent IS the CI Playwright job — the in-sandbox agent cannot download Microsoft-hosted browser binaries (same constraint that has held since Phase 1.B; the cross-runtime CI Playwright job is the authoritative observation per the established Phase 1.B/2.B/3.B/4.B/5.B precedent). It ran chromium + firefox + webkit on commit 9e3a019 and reported green for the diagnostic suite (20 tests × 3 engines = 60 passing assertions), including the 2 new Phase 6.A.2 inventory + pickup assertions: inventory + equipment panels render alongside the canvas; pressing G triggers the pickup action that advances __GAME_STATE_HASH__.
follow_ups_observations: live-domain-https-enforcement: porkchop.github.io/icefall/ continues to 301-redirect to http://www.aaroncaswell.com/icefall/ (plaintext) — same observation carried forward from phase-3.B / 4.B / 5.B. Custom-domain HTTPS enforcement is a Pages settings toggle and remains a Phase 9 release-hardening item; not a Phase 6.B blocker because the deployed bytes and cross-runtime e2e assertions are independent of transport (the CI Playwright job runs against http://127.0.0.1:4173/icefall/ via vite preview, not against the live URL).

phase-6.A.1 transient e2e flakiness: noted in the verification section above; not phase-blocking because Phase 6.A.2's run subsumed and recovered. If Phase 7+ sees recurring flakiness, consider a phase-update adding retries: 1 to playwright.config.ts (currently retries: 0 with forbidOnly: true).

windows-latest cross-OS shard duration: 2m 9s — significantly slower than ubuntu (23s) and macos (17s). Driven by Windows runner provisioning + npm ci + the new 16 atlas recipes. Not blocking; well within the 10-minute job timeout.
follow_ups_carried_forward_from_phase_6_: Code-review N1: inventory count DESC tie-break unreachable through public API; lines 48-51 of inventory.ts are uncovered. Add a unit test that exercises the tie-break path explicitly.
Code-review N2: inventoryRemove validation throw uncovered while symmetric inventoryAdd validation IS tested. Add a unit test asserting the throw path with the pinned error message.
Code-review N3: inventory-replay no-op-heavy sequence — the test could mutate inventory more thoroughly to exercise the replay path harder.

Code-review N4: 3 Phase 3 items (item.stim-patch, item.trauma-pack, item.cyberdeck-mod-1) lack atlas recipes — latent renderer-throw if those items surface on a floor. Address when those items naturally surface (Phase 7 shop NPCs may stock them).
Code-review N5: per-domain index is zero-based per rollBytes call; the inline turn.ts comment could be tightened. Cosmetic.
next_phase: Phase 7 — NPCs, Shops, Boss per docs/PHASES.md:377+. Goal: fill the dungeon with social and climactic content (ripperdoc/fixer NPCs trading eddies for gear; real boss fight on floor 10 with multi-phase deterministic state machine; atlas recipes for ~3 NPC types and the boss). Lead agent: engine-builder (rules) + frontend-builder (UI). Reviewers: code-reviewer + qa-playwright. Acceptance criteria include: scripted run on a fixed fingerprint can buy an upgrade from a ripperdoc, descend to floor 10, and defeat the boss; win-state transition reachable and replayable; all shop transactions resolved deterministically from the state hash (no Math.random for stock generation); full game playable end-to-end on the deployed URL. Phase 7 is NOT a planning-gate phase per docs/PHASES.md:7. The X.A.1/X.A.2/X.B decomposition is the established pattern from Phases 3 / 4 / 5 / 6; Phase 7's first sub-phase will be a drift-detection sweep (likely addressing the Phase 6.A.2 N4 nit on the 3 missing atlas recipes; ARCHITECTURE.md additions for shop-transaction action vocabulary, NPC interaction descriptors, boss FSM contract). Phase 7's atlas regeneration adds ~3 NPC + boss recipes — the cross-OS matrix re-runs against another new atlas binary.
